title: Global Energy Mix Scenario Explorer
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive simulation where students design an energy portfolio using sliders for 8 sources and observe real-time sustainability metrics

learningObjectives: Design an energy portfolio that meets demand constraints while minimizing ecological footprint
Evaluate trade-offs between cost, emissions, land use, and reliability in energy planning
Explain why achieving sustainability requires balancing multiple competing objectives
prerequisites: Basic understanding of renewable and nonrenewable energy sources
Awareness of climate change and CO2 emissions
Familiarity with percentages and proportional reasoning

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Constrained design challenge with real-time feedback
keyQuestions: Why can't we switch to 100% renewable energy overnight?
What happens to grid reliability when you remove all fossil fuels?
Which trade-offs are you willing to accept in your energy portfolio?
How does the current global energy mix compare to a sustainable one?
What role does nuclear energy play in bridging the gap?
commonMisconceptions: Students often assume switching to 100% renewables is straightforward and without trade-offs
Students may not realize that renewables have land use and intermittency challenges
Students may think cost per kWh is the only factor that matters in energy decisions
assessmentOpportunities: Students complete a comparison table for preset scenarios vs. their own design
Students write a reflection on trade-offs encountered during the challenge mode
Students evaluate a hypothetical policy proposal using data from the simulation
